title: Mint Mojito
tagline: Virgin mojito — muddled mint and lime topped with cold club soda. Add white rum if you're not.
macroNotes: Estimated from ingredients — virgin version. Nearly all the calories are the sugar.
Cut it right down by using 1 tbsp sugar per glass instead of 2, or swapping to stevia — takes it to ~25-50 kcal a glass.
Adding 2 oz (60 ml) white rum per glass adds ~130 kcal each. It's a drink, so there's no sensible protein add here — keep it as the refresher it is.

items: 20 fresh mint leaves (~10 per glass), plus sprigs to garnish
1 lime, cut into wedges (half a lime per glass)
4 tbsp sugar (~2 tbsp per glass, to taste)
2 cups (~500 ml) club soda / sparkling water, chilled
Optional: 4 oz (120 ml) white rum — 2 oz per glass

steps.title: Muddle mint and lime
steps.body: Into each sturdy glass put ~10 mint leaves and 2 lime wedges. Muddle gently with a muddler or the back of a spoon to release the mint oils and lime juice — press, don't shred, or the mint turns bitter.

steps.title: Add the sugar
steps.body: Add ~2 tbsp sugar per glass and muddle again to work it into the mint and lime. Adjust to taste if you like it sweeter or sharper.

steps.title: Squeeze in the rest
steps.body: Squeeze the juice from the remaining 2 lime wedges into each glass and drop the spent wedges in.

steps.title: Ice it
steps.body: Fill each glass with ice cubes.

steps.title: Top and stir
steps.body: Add 2 oz white rum per glass if making it boozy, then top with chilled club soda (~1 cup per glass). Stir gently to combine without knocking the fizz out.

steps.title: Garnish and serve
steps.body: Garnish with a mint sprig and a lime wedge, and serve immediately while it's cold and fizzy.

notes: Leave out the rum for a virgin mojito — it's just as refreshing.
Muddle the mint gently; over-crushing releases bitter compounds from the leaves.
Serve straight away — club soda goes flat fast once poured.
source: Adapted from my own cooking notes.
